description: Renders PPTX slides to PNG images via PowerPoint, with optional contact sheet montage. Use when the user says "show me the slides", "what does it look like", wants to preview the PPTX, create thumbnails, or export slides as images. Also used by the validation and comparison workflows.

results.query: I want to see what my deck.pptx actually looks like. Can you render all slides to PNG images?
create a contact sheet montage of all slides in my presentation so I can get a quick overview
render slides 1, 5, and 10 at 300 DPI for print-quality review
export my PowerPoint slides as individual PNG files in a renders/ directory
show me what the slides look like — I want thumbnails of every slide in the deck
generate PNG images from deck.pptx with a 3-column montage for the team to review
I need slide images for a preview email — render at 150 DPI with 480px thumb width
validate my PPTX for layout issues and overflow — check bounds and text overflow
check my deck for overflow and give me a per-slide quality breakdown
compare HTML originals with PPTX output side by side to check fidelity
does the PPTX match the original HTML? Show me a visual diff
run the full end-to-end pipeline including validation and comparison
the colors in my slides are wrong — the alpha blending is off
take a screenshot of an HTML file using Chrome headless
set the quality threshold to 90 for this project
what design rules should I follow for typography and color?
